chief_complaint: A 31-year-old Indonesian female presented with complaints of watery stools, icteric sclerae, jaundice, coughing, and shortness of breath.
history: The patient had a 2-year history of hyperthyroidism with elevated TSH receptor antibodies and was treated with propylthiouracil, with stable remission for a year prior to admission.
exam: Physical examination showed a WHO performance score of 4, delirium, normal blood pressure, tachycardia, tachypnea, axillary temperature of 36.7 degrees C, icteric sclerae, jaundice, exophthalmos, a 3 cm palpable nodule on the right side of the neck, bilateral pulmonary rales, palpable liver, and enlarged spleen.
labs: Laboratory tests showed thrombocytopenia, electrolyte imbalance, hypoalbuminemia, elevated transaminases, suppressed TSH level, elevated free thyroxine (FT4) level, and a positive SARS-CoV-2 PCR swab test.
correct_diagnosis: Thyroid crisis induced by SARS-CoV-2 infection
differentials: Thyroid crisis induced by SARS-CoV-2 infection
correct_ranking: Thyroid crisis induced by SARS-CoV-2 infection
